name: project-structure
description: Organize project folders following industry best practices. Use when setting up new projects, reorganizing codebases, or when folder structure becomes messy. Covers Next.js, Bulletproof React, and FSD patterns.
capabilities: Provide project structure templates for Next.js, Bulletproof React, and FSD patterns
Suggest appropriate folder organization based on project context and team size
Recommend naming conventions for files and folders
Guide folder cleanup workflows using safe practices like legacy folder movement
Create hybrid patterns combining multiple architectural approaches
author: Doyajin174
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance. All detected findings are FALSE POSITIVES - bash commands and URLs are in documentation code blocks, not executable code. The skill provides project structure best practices without any actual code execution, network operations, or file system access.
valueStatement: Messy folder structures slow down development and make codebases hard to navigate. This skill provides proven patterns from Next.js, Bulletproof React, and Feature-Sliced Design to help you organize projects that scale.
userTitle: Organize project folders with best practices
limitations: Does not automatically reorganize folders without user approval
Does not delete files or folders without explicit user commands
Does not execute commands in production environments automatically
Does not validate existing folder structures against all possible patterns

useCases.title: Set up React projects
useCases.description: Choose between Bulletproof React or FSD patterns based on project size and team experience

useCases.title: Organize monorepos
useCases.description: Structure projects with context-based separation for work, personal, and open-source projects

useCases.title: Establish team standards
useCases.description: Define consistent folder structures that scale across multiple projects and team members

promptTemplates.title: New project setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up a project structure for a new React application with the Bulletproof React pattern

promptTemplates.title: Reorganize codebase
promptTemplates.prompt: My project folders are disorganized. Analyze my current structure and suggest how to reorganize using safe migration to _legacy/

promptTemplates.title: Choose architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm building a large React application with 10+ features. Compare Bulletproof React vs Feature-Sliced Design for my use case

promptTemplates.title: Hybrid structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a hybrid project structure combining Next.js App Router with feature-based organization for a dashboard application

bestPractices: Use context-based organization at the root level (work, personal, study) rather than technology-based
Move old code to _legacy/ folders with timestamps instead of deleting
Choose architecture patterns based on team size and project complexity
antiPatterns: Using language or technology as the primary categorization at the project root
Storing projects on desktop or in deeply nested folder structures
Mixing multiple categorization schemes within the same project

faq.question: Which pattern should I choose for a small project?
faq.answer: Start with basic Next.js colocation or a simple feature-based structure. Avoid over-engineering with FSD for small projects.

faq.question: How do I safely clean up legacy code?
faq.answer: Create a _legacy/ folder and move old code there with a date stamp. Never use rm or rm -rf commands. Always verify before moving files.

faq.question: Can this skill work with any framework?
faq.answer: Yes. The principles apply to any project. Specific patterns target React and Next.js, but core organization concepts are universal.

faq.question: Does this skill access my files automatically?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance only. All file operations require explicit user approval through the AI tool being used.

faq.question: What if my project does not fit any pattern?
faq.answer: Combine elements from multiple patterns or create hybrid structures. The skill can help you design custom solutions for your specific needs.

faq.question: How is this different from scaffolding tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance on choosing and evolving structures. It explains why patterns work rather than just generating files.
